meta.role: Program Operations Specialist — Jackson School of International Studies
meta.note: Draft answers in Jamie's voice, adapted from voice corpus. Edit freely before submitting.

answers.question: Why are you interested in this role?
answers.answer: This role sits right at the intersection of two things I love—running programs well and bringing people together. In my current role, a big part of my week is coordinating events and training webinars for 600+ participants: managing speakers, logistics, RSVPs, and all the behind-the-scenes administration that makes them feel effortless. I also handle the documentation and vendor coordination that keep 20+ programs on track. The Program Operations Specialist role blends exactly that—public outreach and events on one side, careful administration and budget tracking on the other. I don't just want to keep things running; I want to help the division's programs reach more people and grow stronger over time. That's the impact I'd hope to bring to the Jackson School.

answers.question: Why UW / this program?
answers.answer: What draws me to UW and the Jackson School is the international mission. I've built my career across borders—working for Taiwanese, American, and Danish companies, in an office of 22 nationalities. So a school whose centers span China, Japan, Korea, and Taiwan, doing real-world work in international education and community outreach, feels close to home. I'm also genuinely drawn to a place where the work has tangible impact—where coordinating a lecture or a K-12 workshop actually connects the community to global scholarship. I'd be excited to support that kind of programming, and to bring my own cross-cultural background to a team that values it.

answers.question: Describe your program operations / coordination experience.
answers.answer: At InGenius Prep, I manage operations for 20+ educational programs and 10+ vendors—coordinating logistics, communications, and delivery end-to-end. I run events and training webinars for 600+ participants, handling speakers, scheduling, and on-the-day logistics. I also build the guides and workflows that keep 70+ cross-functional staff aligned, which cut onboarding time by 75%. Earlier, at Vestas, I streamlined onboarding and administrative processes across three global locations and directed a cross-regional M&A project. My approach is consistent: I diagnose what's actually slowing things down before designing a fix, then build in feedback loops so I can keep improving. My supervisor once described me as 'the most organized person he's ever worked with'—that organization is what keeps complex programs running smoothly.
